expectations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 4: 'Launch one subagent per change. Maximum 5 concurrent agents — if more changes exist, queue them and launch new agents as earlier ones complete.' Agents run with run_in_background: true. The queue-and-refill pattern ensures all N changes are processed.

SKILL.md Step 3: 'Create git worktree: git worktree add /tmp/worktrees/<app>-<change-name> -b feature/<issue-number>/<change-name> origin/development'. Guardrails: 'Worktree isolation: Each change works in /tmp/worktrees/<app>-<change-name> — NEVER modify the main working directory from a subagent.'

SKILL.md Overview: 'proposal → ff (specs/design/tasks) → apply (implement) → verify → browser verify (optional) → archive + feature docs → PR'. Step 4 passes each agent the sub-agent prompt from templates/sub-agent-prompt-template.md which covers the full lifecycle.

Step 5 displays a live progress table with columns: App, Change, Model, Status, Tasks, Quality, Browser, Docs. Step 8 delivers the Final Report with the same table plus a Summary section. Each row shows the per-change outcome.

notes: Parallel subagent spawning, git worktree isolation, full lifecycle delegation, and per-change status reporting are all explicitly specified. The max-5 concurrency cap and queue-and-refill pattern handle large batches.
